query_string: The True Story of Ah Q

tossups.question: The introduction to this story debates whether to call it an official, unauthorized, or supplementary biography. A man in this story reminisces about a wolf’s eyes resembling will-o’-the-wisps. In this story, one man sings “I’ll thrash you with a steel mace,” and drops turnips while being chased by a dog. That character in this story, who has a pile of silver stolen while gambling and gets angry at the words “lamp” or “candle” due to his (*) ringworm scars, is forced to sell a padded quilt after he throws himself at a woman's feet and yells “sleep with me!” At the end of this story, a man is executed after he fails to sign his name on a piece of paper and instead attempts to draw a perfect circle. After losing fights to men like Whiskers Wang, this story’s protagonist slaps himself to claim “spiritual victories.” For 10 points, name this story by Lu Xun which chronicles the life of the title Chinese peasant.
tossups.question_sanitized: The introduction to this story debates whether to call it an official, unauthorized, or supplementary biography. A man in this story reminisces about a wolf's eyes resembling will-o'-the-wisps. In this story, one man sings "I'll thrash you with a steel mace," and drops turnips while being chased by a dog. That character in this story, who has a pile of silver stolen while gambling and gets angry at the words "lamp" or "candle" due to his (*) ringworm scars, is forced to sell a padded quilt after he throws himself at a woman's feet and yells "sleep with me!" At the end of this story, a man is executed after he fails to sign his name on a piece of paper and instead attempts to draw a perfect circle. After losing fights to men like Whiskers Wang, this story's protagonist slaps himself to claim "spiritual victories." For 10 points, name this story by Lu Xun which chronicles the life of the title Chinese peasant.
tossups.answer: The True Story of Ah Q [or Ā Q Zhèngzhuàn]
tossups.answer_sanitized: The True Story of Ah Q [or A Q Zhengzhuan]

tossups.question: This character says “in twenty years I shall be another…” before claiming the eyes of a shouting crowd were more terrible than those of a hungry wolf. A man shouts “turtle’s egg!” after beating this character with a pole. This character, who is cursed to “die sonless” after rubbing the bald head of a nun from the Convent of Quiet Self-Improvement, upsets townspeople by failing to sing a line from an (*) opera before he is executed. A different character with a false pigtail is called “Imitation Foreign Devil” by this character, who attempts to draw a perfect circle while signing a confession. By slapping himself in the face, this character believes he has changed his defeats into “spiritual victories.” For 10 points, name this character, whose “True Story” was told by Lu Xun.
tossups.question_sanitized: This character says "in twenty years I shall be another..." before claiming the eyes of a shouting crowd were more terrible than those of a hungry wolf. A man shouts "turtle's egg!" after beating this character with a pole. This character, who is cursed to "die sonless" after rubbing the bald head of a nun from the Convent of Quiet Self-Improvement, upsets townspeople by failing to sing a line from an (*) opera before he is executed. A different character with a false pigtail is called "Imitation Foreign Devil" by this character, who attempts to draw a perfect circle while signing a confession. By slapping himself in the face, this character believes he has changed his defeats into "spiritual victories." For 10 points, name this character, whose "True Story" was told by Lu Xun.
tossups.answer: Ah Q [accept “The True Story of Ah Q”; prompt on Ah]
tossups.answer_sanitized: Ah Q [accept "The True Story of Ah Q"; prompt on Ah]

tossups.question: In this story, one character sells his quilt in order to raise money to pay a fine. This story features Young D, who takes the protagonist’s jobs after he yells “sleep with me!” at a maidservant. The protagonist of this story is executed after he struggles to think of opera to sing. That character later thinks “only idiots can make (*) perfect circles” after he is forced to sign a piece of paper. Whenever this story’s title protagonist loses a fight, he slaps himself in order to turn those fights into “spiritual victories”. For 10 points, identify this story about a Chinese peasant by Lu Xun.
tossups.answer_sanitized: The True Story of Ah Q
tossups.question_sanitized: In this story, one character sells his quilt in order to raise money to pay a fine. This story features Young D, who takes the protagonist's jobs after he yells "sleep with me!" at a maidservant. The protagonist of this story is executed after he struggles to think of opera to sing. That character later thinks "only idiots can make (*) perfect circles" after he is forced to sign a piece of paper. Whenever this story's title protagonist loses a fight, he slaps himself in order to turn those fights into "spiritual victories". For 10 points, identify this story about a Chinese peasant by Lu Xun.

tossups.question: This character is perturbed when the lice he eats make less of a sound than those eaten by a bewhiskered man. This character is saved from a dog when one of the four turnips he had just stolen falls and startles it. A man yells “turtle’s egg!” while beating up this character for throwing himself at a maid’s feet and asking her to sleep with him. That occurs because this character frequently thinks “Woman…” after he is cursed to be sonless by a little (*) nun whom he accuses of being intimate with a priest. This character antagonizes a man who went to another country and lost his ponytail by calling him a “Foreign Imitation Devil.” This man calls himself an insect and slaps himself in the face to turn his defeats into “spiritual victories.” For 10 points, name this bumbling character who titles a “True Story” by Lu Xun (“loo shwin”).
tossups.answer_sanitized: Ah Q [accept "The True Story of Ah Q"]
tossups.question_sanitized: This character is perturbed when the lice he eats make less of a sound than those eaten by a bewhiskered man. This character is saved from a dog when one of the four turnips he had just stolen falls and startles it. A man yells "turtle's egg!" while beating up this character for throwing himself at a maid's feet and asking her to sleep with him. That occurs because this character frequently thinks "Woman..." after he is cursed to be sonless by a little (*) nun whom he accuses of being intimate with a priest. This character antagonizes a man who went to another country and lost his ponytail by calling him a "Foreign Imitation Devil." This man calls himself an insect and slaps himself in the face to turn his defeats into "spiritual victories." For 10 points, name this bumbling character who titles a "True Story" by Lu Xun ("loo shwin").

bonuses.leadin: A man passes out in his son’s college dorm while frantically searching for a place to use the bathroom in a story originally written in this language, titled “Piss and Pride.” For 10 points each:
parts: Identify this language, many of whose modern poets use the eight-syllable metrical line from an epic poem whose hero wins his kingdom by riding his magical steed to victory in a horse race.

Tsering Dondrup’s story about a lazy everyman, “Ralo,” prompted an examination of Tibetan national character similar to the one in China occasioned by this novella, whose protagonist rationalizes all of his mishaps as “spiritual victories.”

Another modern Tibetan author, Jamyang Norbu, is best known for a novel in which Sherlock Holmes joins forces with Hurree Babu, a spy who directly supervises the title orphan of this Rudyard Kipling novel.
answers: Tibetan [or Standard Tibetan; or Classical Tibetan; or Lhasa Tibetan; or Bod skad; or Böké; or Lha-sa'i skad; or Lhaséké; the epic is The Epic of King Gesar]
The True Story of Ah Q [or Ā Q Zhèngzhuàn] (by Lu Xun)
answers_sanitized: Tibetan [or Standard Tibetan; or Classical Tibetan; or Lhasa Tibetan; or Bod skad; or Boke; or Lha-sa'i skad; or Lhaseke; the epic is The Epic of King Gesar]
The True Story of Ah Q [or A Q Zhengzhuan] (by Lu Xun)

Another modern Tibetan author, Jamyang Norbu, is best known for a novel in which Sherlock Holmes joins forces with Hurree Babu, a spy who directly supervises the title orphan of this Rudyard Kipling novel.

bonuses.leadin: In this novel, a man wins many "spiritual victories" even while he is being humiliated and frets over drawing a perfect circle to sign a confession. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this Lu Xun novel about the "adventures" of a peasant, who eventually gets executed.
Lu Xun is a novelist from this Asian country. Other authors from this country include Ba Jin and Mo Yan.
A Chinese form of poetry called jueju consists of this many five or seven-syllable lines. China is also considered to have this many “Great Classics,” one of which is Journey to the West.
answers_sanitized: The True Story of Ah Q

bonuses.leadin: The protagonist of this story encounters a gang of punks who make lewd comments about using the title object on a young beggar-girl. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this 1924 short story in which Si-Ming buys the title scented hygienic object for his wife.

The title character is executed at the end of this short story, after plotting to use the Republican revolution for vengeance on his enemies and praising himself for "moral victories." It was the first story written in vernacular Mandarin after the start of the May 4th Movement.
This author of "Regret for the Past" and "Medicine" wrote "Soap" and "The True Story of Ah Q."
